content.text:
Build an Android-only secure personal Vehicle Technician Job Tracker app called TechTimes for a single technician. Fully offline and GDPR-safe: store ONLY vehicle registration numbers + job/work data (no customer names, addresses, phone numbers, emails).

Visual style + backgrounds (must be consistent everywhere)

Every screen uses automotive car repair / workshop themed backgrounds (tools, workshop bays, engine bay, diagnostics, ramps).

Provide two global themes:

Dark Workshop (default)

Light Workshop

Settings toggle to switch Dark/Light globally.

Always apply a subtle overlay for readability; include an “Overlay Strength” slider in Settings.

Modern, clean, card-based UI with excellent readability and smooth feel.

Security: PIN + Biometrics (Android)
PIN

App opens to a secure login.

Default PIN: 3101.

PIN can be changed in Settings (must enter current PIN).

Add toggle: “Lock on resume” (default ON). If enabled, require login after returning from background.

Biometrics

Add Biometric login (fingerprint/face if available on Android).

On login screen show: “Use fingerprint/biometrics” button if supported.

Add Settings toggle: Enable Biometrics (default OFF).

If enabled, allow biometric unlock as an alternative to PIN.

Security rules:

Biometrics must unlock only if the device biometrics are enrolled.

If biometric fails/cancelled, fall back to PIN entry.

Changing PIN does not disable biometrics, but require PIN for high-security actions (changing PIN, restoring backup).

Core job tracking (AW system)

Use AW / AWs everywhere (never FRU).

1 AW = 5 minutes
Each Job record stores:

WIP number (exactly 5 digits, required, validate)

Vehicle registration (required, auto-uppercase)

AW value dropdown 0–100

Notes optional

Date & time saved auto-captured
Calculated display:

Minutes = AW × 5

Show time as hh:mm and decimal hours (2dp)

Main screens / features
1) Dashboard

Cards showing:

Today: jobs count, total AW, total time (hh:mm), average AW/job

This Week: total jobs, AW, hours

This Month: total jobs, AW, hours
Quick buttons: Add Job, View Jobs, Stats

2) Add Job

Clean entry window:

WIP, Reg, AW dropdown, Notes

Live calculated time display

Save validates + stores job + shows “Job saved”

After save: clear fields and remain on Add Job

Default behaviour: “Save & Add Another”

3) Jobs (history)

Newest-first list

Row shows: WIP, Reg, AW, time, saved date/time

Search by WIP or Reg

Filters: Today / Week / Month / Custom range

Job Details: view, edit (validated), delete (confirm)

4) Stats

Month selector

For selected month: total jobs, AW, hours, average AW/job

Weekly breakdown (week 1–5 totals)

Optional simple charts if easy (not required)

5) Settings + data tools

Change PIN (current PIN required)

Enable Biometrics toggle

Lock on resume toggle

Background Theme: Dark/Light

Overlay Strength slider

Export CSV (columns: createdAt, wipNumber, vehicleReg, aw, minutes, notes) using share/save

Backup & Restore:

Backup creates a local file containing all jobs + settings

Restore imports that file with validation + confirmation to overwrite

Quality rules

Inputs must never hide under the keyboard.

Fast with large lists.

Friendly validation messages (e.g., “WIP must be 5 digits”).

Handle empty states gracefully.

Backgrounds across ALL screens

Every screen must use car-repair/workshop images as background.

Support 2 modes: Dark Workshop (default) and Light Workshop.

Settings: global toggle Dark/Light + Overlay Strength slider (0–60%) for readability.

Background should never make text unreadable; use cards with blur/semi-transparent surfaces.

Dark mode quality

Dark mode must be “clean and consistent” across all pages, like the settings page standard. 
apppppp


PROMPT 2/4 — Perfect AW job tracking + scanning (camera + gallery) + fast entry UX
Implement flawless technician job logging (offline only; store only reg + job data).

Job fields + rules

WIP Number = exactly 5 digits (required).

Vehicle Reg (required, auto-uppercase).

AW dropdown 0–100; 1 AW = 5 minutes (use AW/AWs everywhere).

Notes optional. Auto-save timestamp.

Live time calculation (minutes + hours) while typing.

Add Job UX

Add “Save Another” button next to the Scan button on the right side so user doesn’t close keyboard to save. 
apppppp


Save must always work + show animated confirmations (“Record Saved”, errors, etc.). 
apppppp


Scanning

“Scan Reg” (camera + pick from gallery), OCR, auto-detect UK reg, user confirms.

“Scan Job Card” (camera + gallery) OCR and extract:

WIP No = job number

Reg No = vehicle reg

Show confirmation sheet with extracted values + alternatives; then autofill Add Job.

PROMPT 3/4 — The stats system: circles, efficiency, target hours, absences, drill-down
Build the full performance analytics system with blue as the main accent, with progress circles and detailed drill-down pages.

Monthly target

Default monthly target = 180 hours.

“Sold hours” is derived from total AWs for the month.

First dashboard circle = sold hours vs target hours, shows hours remaining.

Efficiency

Efficiency = (Sold Hours / Available Hours) × 100.

Available Hours = weekdays Mon–Fri × 8.5h/day, minus absences (if configured).

Color ranges:

Green: 65–100%

Yellow/Orange: 31–64%

Red: 0–30%

Efficiency circle must also show total available hours value.

Absence logger (must be powerful)

Add absence logging with:

Days count + half-day/full-day

A third dropdown “Deduction Type” with two options:

Deduct from Monthly Target Hours

Deduct from Total Available Hours (efficiency) 
apppppp


Must reset month-to-month correctly.

Clickable drill-down

Tapping the Target circle opens a detailed stats page showing target, sold hours, remaining, totals. 
apppppp


Tapping Efficiency circle opens a detailed breakdown showing the exact numbers + formula used. 
apppppp


Job Records

Search + filter-by-field + results counter + summary stats for filtered results.

## 📅 Calendar Features

**Multi-View Calendar System:**
- **Day View**: Shows detailed efficiency and progress circles for a single day, with job list
- **Week View**: Horizontal scrollable week with mini circles for each day (Monday-Sunday)
- **Month View**: Grid calendar with tiny dual circles (efficiency + progress) on each day, plus month-level summary circles
- **Year View**: 12-month grid showing all months with navigation to drill down

**Live Data Sync:**
- Real-time efficiency calculations based on sold hours vs available hours
- Progress tracking against daily/monthly targets
- Color-coded efficiency indicators (green 65%+, yellow 31-64%, red 0-30%)
- Updates automatically as jobs are added throughout the day

**Navigation:**
- Swipe/tap to navigate between periods
- "Today" button to jump to current date
- Tap any day to zoom into day view
- Seamless transitions between view modes

## 📊 Enhanced Export Functionality

- **Daily Export**: Select specific day, shows that day's jobs and totals
- **Weekly Export**: Select week, shows each day with jobs + day totals, then week total (Monday-Saturday)
- **Monthly Export**: Select month, shows each day with jobs + day totals, then month total
- **Entire Export**: Shows all data with day totals, week totals, and month totals grouped hierarchically

**Export Formats:**
- **PDF**: Stylish formatted report with technician name, date, and color-coded sections
- **JSON**: Standard format for backup/import with job data

**Grouping Structure:**
- Jobs grouped by day with day totals
- Days grouped by week (Monday-Saturday) with week totals
- Weeks grouped by month with month totals
- Clear visual separators and color-coded sections

## 🎨 Visual Design

**Calendar Circles:**
- Outer circle: Efficiency (green/yellow/red based on performance)
- Inner circle: Progress toward target (blue)
- Different sizes for different views (140px day, 50px week, 24px month)
- Month view shows both circles overlapped for compact display

**Export Modal:**
- Clean date/week/month selector
- Previous/Next/Today navigation buttons
- Export type toggle (Daily/Weekly/Monthly/All)
- Separate buttons for PDF and JSON export

The calendar is accessible from Settings → View Calendar, and the enhanced export is available from Settings → Export Reports with full date/week/month selection capabilities!

content.text: Update the app’s internal calculation system so that it automatically determines technician efficiency using live date logic and AW-based job data. The app must calculate: Total Available Hours — based on weekdays only (Mon–Fri). Total Sold Hours — based on all jobs entered (AW totals converted to hours). Efficiency % — using the ratio of sold hours to available hours for the current month. 🧠 Logic Explanation 1. Total Available Hours The app should automatically determine how many working days exist in the current month from the 1st day up to today’s date. Only Monday to Friday are counted — all Saturdays and Sundays are ignored. Each weekday represents 8.5 hours of available working time (this includes a 30-minute lunch break already deducted from a 9-hour day). Therefore, Total Available Hours = (Number of Weekdays) × 8.5 hours. This value updates automatically every day as the month progresses. 2. Total Sold Hours Each job entered into the system has an AW (Allocated Work Unit) value. 1 AW equals 5 minutes, or 0.0833 hours. The app must convert each job’s AW value into hours, then sum all job hours for the current month to get Total Sold Hours. Example: 840 AW in total = 840 × 0.0833 = 69.97 sold hours. 3. Efficiency Calculation Once both totals are known, efficiency should be calculated automatically as: Efficiency % = (Total Sold Hours ÷ Total Available Hours) × 100 The percentage should be rounded to the nearest whole number. If Total Available Hours = 0 (for example, before the month starts), efficiency should default to 0%. 4. Display & Updates Efficiency and all related metrics should refresh automatically whenever: A new job is added or edited. The device date changes to a new day or month. The summary area should clearly show: Total AW Total Sold Hours (in hours) Total Available Hours (in hours) Efficiency % 5. Visual Indicators (Optional) Add color feedback for quick performance insight: 🟩 Green: 90% or higher (excellent) 🟨 Yellow: 75–89% (average) 🟥 Red: below 75% (needs improvement) 6. Reset Logic At the start of each new month, totals and efficiency automatically reset, beginning a fresh count from the 1st day of that month. 🧩 Example Scenario From the 1st to today, there are 17 working days (Monday–Friday). → Total Available Hours = 17 × 8.5 = 144.5 hours Technician logged 840 AW this month. → Total Sold Hours = 840 × 0.0833 = 69.97 hours Efficiency = (69.97 ÷ 144.5) × 100 = 48% Command to AI Developer: Implement this exact logic in the app’s calculation engine. Perfect! Now I've implemented a comprehensive work schedule management system. Here's what I've created:

## ✅ Implementation Summary

### 1. **New Work Schedule Editor Page** (`app/edit-work-schedule.tsx`)
- **Select Working Days**: Choose any combination of days (Mon-Sun) with visual day buttons
- **Set Working Hours**: Customize start time, end time, and lunch break
- **Real-time Calculations**: See daily, weekly, and monthly hours update live
- **Validation**: Ensures valid time formats and logical schedules
- **Formula Display**: Shows how efficiency is calculated

### 2. **Enhanced Storage** (`utils/offlineStorage.ts`)
- Added `workingDays` array (e.g., [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] for Mon-Fri)
- Added `startTime` and `endTime` (HH:MM format)
- Added `lunchBreakMinutes` for unpaid break time
- Maintains backward compatibility with existing data

### 3. **Updated Calculation Logic** (`utils/api.ts`)
- **`getWorkingDaysInMonth()`**: Counts only selected working days in a month
- **`getWorkingDaysToDate()`**: Counts working days from month start to today
- **Dynamic Available Hours**: Calculated as `Working Days × Daily Hours`
- **Efficiency Formula**: `(Sold Hours ÷ Available Hours) × 100`
- **Updated Color Thresholds**: 
  - 🟩 Green: 90%+ (excellent)
  - 🟨 Yellow: 75-89% (average)
  - 🟥 Red: <75% (needs improvement)

### 4. **Settings Page Integration** (`app/(tabs)/settings.tsx`)
- Added "Edit Work Schedule" button with icon
- Shows current schedule summary (days, times, hours/day)
- Helpful hint text explaining the feature

### 5. **Calendar Updates** (`app/calendar.tsx`)
- Uses custom working days for all calculations
- Only counts selected days as "working days"
- Efficiency circles reflect actual schedule

## 🎯 Key Features

### Flexible Schedule Options
- **4-day work week**: Select Mon-Thu, 7am-6pm
- **Part-time**: Select Mon, Wed, Fri only
- **Weekend work**: Include Saturday or Sunday
- **Custom hours**: Any start/end time with lunch break

### Automatic Calculations
- **Total Available Hours** = (Number of Working Days from month start to today) × Daily Hours
- **Total Sold Hours** = Sum of all AW × 0.0833 hours (1 AW = 5 minutes)
- **Efficiency %** = (Sold Hours ÷ Available Hours) × 100

### Example Scenarios

**Scenario 1: Standard 5-day week**
- Working Days: Mon-Fri
- Hours: 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM (11 hours - 0.5 lunch = 10.5h/day)
- 17 working days this month = 178.5 available hours

**Scenario 2: 4-day week**
- Working Days: Mon-Thu
- Hours: 7:00 AM - 6:00 PM (10.5h/day)
- 14 working days this month = 147 available hours

**Scenario 3: Part-time**
- Working Days: Mon, Wed, Fri
- Hours: 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM (6 hours - 0 lunch = 6h/day)
- 10 working days this month = 60 available hours

## 📊 How It Works

1. **User customizes schedule** in Edit Work Schedule page
2. **App counts working days** from 1st of month to today
3. **Calculates available hours** = Working Days × Daily Hours
4. **Tracks sold hours** from all jobs entered (AW × 0.0833)
5. **Displays efficiency** = (Sold ÷ Available) × 100
6. **Updates automatically** every day as month progresses

All efficiency calculations throughout the app (dashboard, calendar, stats) now use this customizable schedule! 🎉

### Key Implementation Details:

**Week Calculation Logic:**
```typescript
// Sunday = 0, Monday = 1, ..., Saturday = 6
function getWeekStart(date: Date = new Date()): Date {
  const d = new Date(date);
  const day = d.getDay(); // 0 = Sunday
  const diff = day; // Days since Sunday
  d.setDate(d.getDate() - diff);
  d.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0);
  return d;
}
```

**Live Sync:**
- Dashboard refreshes stats every 30 seconds automatically
- Week stats are recalculated on each refresh
- When Sunday arrives, the week counter resets and starts tracking the new week
